_doc: Classification registry for armed-valve-audit.py (RETRO-0708-ARMED-VALVE-AUDIT; PREC-2026-07-08-armed-valve-outcome). The audit derives valves from beat-source gates and generic sensors.yaml capabilities. This file holds only non-sensor classifications and external probes (a URL that must serve, an artifact that must carry the rail). A discovered gate classified nowhere surfaces as UNCLASSIFIED — new valves self-surface, the registry never silently lags the code.

deliverable.what: outward dispatch of queued tasks to vendor lanes — the queue's entire purpose; permanently off means the board pools work nothing executes
censor residuals mirrored to public GitHub issues (retro finding 8: ran every beat in permanent dry-run; 4 residuals never mirrored)
the sovereign mint reachable and serving its licence pubkey — the Buy-Pro rail's origin (retro finding 4: deployed URL returned nothing)
the public exporter install artifact carries the mint checkout — the user-reachable half of the rail (EXCAV-0708-EXPORTER-ARM ship-gate)

_safety_note: LIMEN_DISPATCH_ASYNC / LIMEN_CENSOR_APPLY / LIMEN_MEDIA_ATOMIZE are armed in the DAEMON'S environment (launchd plist), which this audit cannot read from a shell — promoting one to 'deliverable' requires a probe of daemon truth (a runtime stamp), not an env read; enactment-audit.py owns declared-vs-live for fleet flags. LIMEN_ALLOW_HIGH_LOCAL_LIMIT is a budget-cap override: off IS the design.
